---
title: "Analysis"
author: "Melle Stephen Oudshoorn"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r load packages}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(modelsummary)
library(psych)
library(fixest)

eu_countries <- c(
  "Austria", "Belgium", "Bulgaria", "Croatia", "Cyprus", "Czech Republic",
  "Denmark", "Estonia", "Finland", "France", "Germany", "Greece", "Hungary",
  "Ireland", "Italy", "Latvia", "Lithuania", "Luxembourg", "Malta", "Netherlands",
  "Poland", "Portugal", "Romania", "Slovakia", "Slovenia", "Spain", "Sweden"
)

high_emission_sectors <- c( #division of high emissions sectors
      "Oil, Gas & Consumable Fuels",
      "Energy Equipment & Services",
      "Metals & Mining", 
      "Chemicals",
      "Paper & Forest Products",
      "Construction Materials",
      "Electric Utilities",
      "Gas Utilities",
      "Water Utilities",
      "Multi-Utilities",
      "Independent Power and Renewable Electricity Producers"
    )
```

```{r setup the basics}
df_panel <- read.csv("data/CSRD_thesis_data/panel_final.csv", check.names = FALSE) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(esg_score)) %>%  #filter out NA's for the ESG score
  filter(`GICS Sector Name` != "Financials") %>% #filter out the financial sector due to unreliable emissions
  filter(year >= 2014, year <= 2025) %>% #2026 and 2013 were in the original dataset but also too sparse
  mutate(
    leverage = total_debt_actual/market_capitalization, #create leverage variable
    roa = net_income_after_tax / total_assets, #create roa variable
    high_emission_sector = `GICS Industry Name` %in% high_emission_sectors, #defines high emission sectors
    is_listed_eu = `Country of Exchange` %in% eu_countries, #defines whether a company is listed in the eu
    #size standards
    has_high_employees = ifelse(is.na(number_of_employees), FALSE, number_of_employees > 500), #whether a company exceeds NFRD size threshold regarding employees
    has_high_assets = ifelse(is.na(total_assets), FALSE, total_assets > 20000000), #whether a company exceeds NFRD size threshold regarding assets
    has_high_turnover = ifelse(is.na(turnover), FALSE, turnover > 40000000), #whether a company exceeds NFRD size threshold regarding turnover
    nfrd_size = has_high_employees, #if they have larger than 500 employees they are exposed
    #eu domicile
    in_EU_EU_domicile = `Country of Incorporation` %in% eu_countries,
    #NFRD/CSRD exposed
    treatment_exposed = (has_high_employees | has_high_assets | has_high_turnover) & #adheres to size rules
      (is_listed_eu | in_EU_EU_domicile),  #is in the EU
    #policy shock
    post_csrd = year >= 2024,
    #emissions
    total_emissions_log = log(scope_1_and_scope_2_and_scope_3_combined_reported_or_estimated_total + 1),
    scope1_emissions_log = log(scope_1_combined_reported_or_estimated_total + 1),
    scope2_emissions_log = log(scope_2_combined_reported_or_estimated_total + 1),
    scope3_emissions_log = log(scope_3_combined_reported_or_estimated_total + 1),
    #other pollutants
    nox_log = log(nox_emissions + 1),
    sox_log = log(sox_emissions + 1),
    waste_log = log(hazardous_waste + 1),
    #standardise emissions to match
    total_emissions_std = as.numeric(scale(total_emissions_log)),
    scope1_emissions_std = as.numeric(scale(scope1_emissions_log)),
    scope2_emissions_std = as.numeric(scale(scope2_emissions_log)),
    scope3_emissions_std = as.numeric(scale(scope3_emissions_log)),
    nox_std = as.numeric(scale(nox_log)),
    sox_std = as.numeric(scale(sox_log)),
    waste_std = as.numeric(scale(waste_log)),
    esg_std = as.numeric(scale(esg_score)),
    env_pillar_std = as.numeric(scale(environmental_pillar_score))
  )

environmental_vars <- c(
  #emissions
  "scope_1_combined_reported_or_estimated_total",
  "scope_2_combined_reported_or_estimated_total",
  "scope_3_combined_reported_or_estimated_total",
  "nox_emissions",
  "sox_emissions",
  "hazardous_waste",
  #efficiency
  "energy_use_total",
  "renewable_energy_use_ratio",
  #waste & pollution
  "waste_recycled_to_total_waste",
  "water_discharged",
  #environmental footpring
  "water_withdrawal_total",
  "environmental_restoration_initiatives", #binary (TRUE/FALSE)
  "research_development_expense",
  "biodiversity_due_diligence"
)

social_vars <- c(
  #workforce
  "number_of_employees",
  "women_employees",
  "women_managers",
  "training_hours_total",
  "health_safety_policy", #binary (TRUE/FALSE)
  "salary_gap",
  "gender_pay_gap_percentage",
  #human rights
  "human_rights_policy", #binary (TRUE/FALSE)
  "csr_sustainability_external_audit", #binary (TRUE/FALSE)
  "supply_chain_health_safety_improvements",
  #community
  "donations_total"
)

governance_vars <- c(
  #shareholders
  "shareholders_score",
  "shareholder_approval_significant_transactions", #binary (TRUE/FALSE)
  "independent_board_members",
  #CSR strategy
  "csr_sustainability_reporting", #binary (TRUE/FALSE)
  "csr_sustainability_committee", #binary (TRUE/FALSE)
  #management
  "corruption_oversight", #binary (TRUE/FALSE)
  "stakeholder_engagement", #binary (TRUE/FALSE)
  "audit_committee_independence",
  "policy_executive_compensation_esg_performance"
)

all_vars <- c(environmental_vars, social_vars, governance_vars)
```

```{r build the disclosure score}
construct_disclosure_scores <- function(df, environmental_vars, social_vars, governance_vars) {
  
  # Helper: score a single variable
  score_var <- function(x) {
    # Detect binary (logical or only TRUE/FALSE/NA values)
    is_binary <- is.logical(x) || all(x %in% c(TRUE, FALSE, 1, 0, NA))
    
    if (is_binary) {
      # Binary: TRUE/1 → 1, FALSE/0 → 0, NA → 0
      return(as.integer(!is.na(x) & as.logical(x)))
    } else {
      # Numerical: reported (non-NA) → 1, NA → 0
      return(as.integer(!is.na(x)))
    }
  }
  
  all_vars <- c(environmental_vars, social_vars, governance_vars)
  
  # Score each variable group
  env_scores <- df[environmental_vars] %>% mutate(across(everything(), score_var))
  soc_scores <- df[social_vars]        %>% mutate(across(everything(), score_var))
  gov_scores <- df[governance_vars]    %>% mutate(across(everything(), score_var))
  
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      environmental_disclosure_score = rowSums(env_scores),
      social_disclosure_score        = rowSums(soc_scores),
      governance_disclosure_score    = rowSums(gov_scores),
      esg_disclosure_score           = environmental_disclosure_score +
                                       social_disclosure_score +
                                       governance_disclosure_score,
      
      # Max possible scores per pillar
      env_max = length(environmental_vars),
      soc_max = length(social_vars),
      gov_max = length(governance_vars),
      esg_max = length(all_vars),
      
      # Normalised 0–1 scores
      environmental_disclosure_score_norm = environmental_disclosure_score / env_max,
      social_disclosure_score_norm        = social_disclosure_score        / soc_max,
      governance_disclosure_score_norm    = governance_disclosure_score    / gov_max,
      esg_disclosure_score_norm           = esg_disclosure_score           / esg_max
    ) %>%
    select(-env_max, -soc_max, -gov_max, -esg_max)  # drop helpers
  
  return(df)
}

df_panel <- construct_disclosure_scores(
  df_panel,
  environmental_vars,
  social_vars,
  governance_vars
)
```

```{r describe the disclosure variables}
psych::describe(df_panel %>%
  select(
    esg_disclosure_score,
    environmental_disclosure_score,
    social_disclosure_score,
    governance_disclosure_score
  ))

df_esg_disclosure_long <- df_panel %>%
  select(
    esg_disclosure_score,
    environmental_disclosure_score,
    social_disclosure_score,
    governance_disclosure_score
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "value"
  )

ggplot(df_esg_disclosure_long, aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50) +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of ESG Variables",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r computing the greenwashing index according to yu et al.}
#standardise disclosure and performance scores within industry-year groups
df_panel <- df_panel %>% 
  group_by(`GICS Sector Name`, year) %>%  #ensures standardisation happens within each industry-year cluster
  mutate(
    #standardise the disclosure scores within industry-year groups
    esg_disclosure_z = ((esg_disclosure_score - mean(esg_disclosure_score, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(esg_disclosure_score, na.rm = TRUE)),
    environmental_disclosure_z = ((environmental_disclosure_score - mean(environmental_disclosure_score, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(environmental_disclosure_score, na.rm = TRUE)),
    social_disclosure_z = ((social_disclosure_score - mean(social_disclosure_score, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(social_disclosure_score, na.rm = TRUE)),
    governance_disclosure_z = ((governance_disclosure_score - mean(governance_disclosure_score, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(governance_disclosure_score, na.rm = TRUE)),
    #standardise performance scores
    esg_performance_z = (esg_score - mean(esg_score, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(esg_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    environmental_performance_z = (environmental_pillar_score - mean(environmental_pillar_score, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(environmental_pillar_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    social_performance_z = (social_pillar_score - mean(social_pillar_score, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(social_pillar_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    governance_performance_z = (governance_pillar_score - mean(governance_pillar_score, na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(governance_pillar_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    #compute greenwashing index (standardised disclosure - standardised performance)
    greenwashing_esg = esg_disclosure_z - esg_performance_z,
    greenwashing_environmental = environmental_disclosure_z - environmental_performance_z,
    greenwashing_social = social_disclosure_z - social_performance_z,
    greenwashing_governance = governance_disclosure_z - governance_performance_z
  ) %>% 
  ungroup()
```

```{r computing the greenwashing index according to Dorfleitner & Utz}
#esg vs total ghg
gw_dorfleitner_utz_model <- feols(esg_std ~ total_emissions_std | `Identifier (RIC)` + year, data = df_panel)
#E-pillar of esg vs total ghg
gw_env_dorfleitner_utz_model <-  feols(environmental_pillar_score ~ total_emissions_std | `Identifier (RIC)` + year, data = df_panel)

#compute the greenwashing as the residual
df_panel$gw_dorfleitner_utz <- ifelse(is.na(df_panel$total_emissions_std), 
                                     0, pmax(0, residuals(gw_dorfleitner_utz_model))) #either 0, or higher than 0 greenwashing
df_panel$gw_env_dorfleitner_utz <- ifelse(is.na(df_panel$total_emissions_std), 
                                         0, pmax(0, residuals(gw_env_dorfleitner_utz_model)))
```

# Standard descriptive statistics

```{r colnames}
colnames(df_panel)
```

# Sample structure

```{r basic panel structure}
# number of firms, years, observations
df_panel %>%
  summarise(
    n_obs = n(),
    n_firms = n_distinct(`ISIN Code`),
    n_years = n_distinct(year)
  )

# observations per year
df_panel %>%
  count(year) %>%
  arrange(year)
```

```{r N observations by country, treatment status, and GICS sector}
# observations per country
df_panel %>%
  count(`Country of Incorporation`) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>% 
  head(20)

#observations per treatment status
df_panel %>% 
  count(treatment_exposed)

#observations per GICS sector
df_panel %>%
  count(`GICS Sector Name`) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>% 
  head(20)
```



# Missing values

```{r}
df_panel %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~mean(is.na(.))))
```



# Difference-in-differences

```{r did computation}
did_model <- feols(
  greenwashing_esg ~ treatment_exposed * post_csrd |
    `GICS Industry Name` + year,
  data = df_panel,
  cluster = ~`GICS Industry Name`
)

summary(did_model)
```
